title: My Daughter is Struggling | #shorts
full_text: Okay, Zoya, forget that I'm your mother. I am extremely objective and a very solid advice giver and I'm going to solve whatever your problem is right now. I'm 21 and I just feel like I'm getting so old. Yeah, that's right. How do I meet somebody and, you know, share all my experiences with someone? Right there feels like the root of the problem because aren't I supposed to fall in love and no find my partner? Then they fall out of love because that's the process of falling. I'm just worried I'm never even going to have that experience because I'm just so ambitious and loud and opinionated. I feel like the Indian community thinks I'm too much. I mean, they wouldn't be, you know, you think I'm too much. I mean, it would help you if you had less. You're supposed to be my therapist. I just want to be heard and validated. I don't care about solutions. No. What the validated? No one. What are you? A parking ticket? I just watch all these movies. And I read all these books and like the point of life is love. The people who do adventures are reality TV show people who do them because they're getting paid. The whole purpose of life is to get paid. You're going to make money. You're going to control your opinions so other people like you a little bit. My mom doesn't even believe I'm going to meet somebody. I know your mom and yes, she does have concerns.
